name: Tijuana (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field of the flag — purity, honesty, respect and peace
elements of the coat of arms — strength, bravery and courage
the eagle in the coat of arms — the colour of the lands of Baja California
details of the coat of arms

description: A white field charged in the centre with the municipal coat of arms of Tijuana.
history: Tijuana, the largest city of Baja California, has no formally adopted municipal flag. In practice it flies a white field bearing its municipal coat of arms — designed by architect Homero Martínez de Hoyos and journalist Salvador Zapata after a 1963 contest organised by the municipal council. The arms were adopted by the city the same year and the flag has been in widespread use since.

features.symbolism: plain white field bearing the municipal coat of arms

features.role: coat of arms of the Municipality of Tijuana (1963)
features.symbolism: designed by architect Homero Martínez de Hoyos and journalist Salvador Zapata after a 1963 contest; the eagle and red and brown elements represent power, the lands of Baja California and the city's character; the arms have been the city's emblem since 1963
